issues: 1x silhouette is very small and barely readable — flower shape is a blurry smudge of pink
petals lack strong outer outline, causing mild mush on dark bg
personality is present but not strongly distinct from generic flower icon
reasoning: At 4x the icon reads clearly as a cute pixel-art flower: round pink petals, green stem with leaves, friendly toy-like proportions that pass the sticker test. Charm is genuine — the pixel style and petal arrangement feel soft and approachable rather than edgy. On the dark background (4x DARK) it holds up well with sufficient contrast from the pink/green palette. On the light background (4x LIGHT) it is slightly softer but still legible. The critical weakness is 1x readability: the icon collapses to an indistinct pink-and-dark smudge where no recognizable flower silhouette survives. Edge cleanliness at 4x is acceptable for pixel art but the outer ring of petals has no dark outline to lock the silhouette against both backgrounds. Charming at zoom but silhouette clarity fails the 1x rule.
